text: In this case, I present a forty-two-year-old female, gravita two, para two, with a history of abnormal uterine bleeding, pelvic pain, and a right complex ovarian mass measuring eight by six centimeters. She has a past surgical history of a laparoscopic cholecystectomy, cesarean section times two, and a bilateral tubal ligation.
Procedure performed was a robotic assisted total laparoscopic hysterectomy, bilateral salpingectomy, right oophorectomy, incidental appendectomy secondary to adhesions, and water cystoscopy.

The surgeon demonstrates the technique of performing an appendectomy through the natural vaginal orifice, avoiding the need to enlarge a trocar site for the insertion of a 12mm echelon flexible stapler.

Yes, there are other methods to perform the appendectomy, like using endo loops, for example. but I chose not to enlarge other trocar sites to accommodate the twanglometer stapler. The stapler is my preference for appendectomy in GYN oncology procedures.
The surgeon uses a vaso-sealing device to secure the meso-appendix, effectively sealing the appendiceal artery and skeletonizing the appendix to the cecum.

The surgeon uses an Echelon 45mm flexible stapler with a blue load to perform the appendectomy through the vaginal orifice following the hysterectomy. The appendix is removed due to adhesions to the right ovarian complex mass, which resulted in an endometrioma.
in this fashion there's no need to use an additional assistant port or access port and there is no need to enlarge smaller trocar sites
The surgeon uses a grasper to deliver the appendix through the vaginal orifice. The ileocecal junction is visually inspected for patency and hemostasis.
The surgeon completes the robotic appendectomy through the vaginal orifice, utilizing the stapler to remove the appendix.
